title: Review: pre-merge code review (all backends)
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — one real correctness bug (file put non-atomic write could destroy a secret on disk-full), one explicit rule violation (non-Zeroizing copy in file put), one misleading comment. All other findings are test gaps or first-call latency edge cases. No security vulnerabilities, no memory unsafety, no logic inversions found.
Confidence: 85% chance of no missed bugs. Uncertainty driven by: (a) bitwarden and doppler not integration-tested — network-layer behavior and actual API response shapes unverified; (b) pkcs11 CKA_LABEL behavior with multi-slash labels on real tokens vs SoftHSM untested; (c) aws-kms integration tests sign but don't verify — DER conversion is unit-tested with synthetic vectors but not against a real KMS signature. Would raise confidence if: aws-kms integration test decoded the public key and called a real ECDSA verifier on the returned signature.
